signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.2; 160.00 remains the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. BoJ expected to hold rates steady at 0.75% in April. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — watch 157.00 as the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7154 — CFTC LF net long +41,675 (72% long). HV30 at 6.5%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 8.2% — options pricing a 1.7 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.08: mild put skew. Price action stalling at 0.7154; late-trend caution signal. Watch for LF delta turning negative to invalidate.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Technical
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1763 — EUR LF net short −72 (50% long). Price testing 1.1750 support; ECB's Guindos sees private credit as key risk to financial stability. ECB rate at 2% vs Fed 3.75% — 175bp spread favors USD. Watch 1.1700 for a potential breakdown.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3503 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.
